title: Broken Access Control / IDOR
principle: Authorize every request against the specific object it touches, on the server, using the session identity — never a user-supplied id or role. Don't assume that because the UI hid an action, the endpoint is safe; check ownership or permission on each read and write. Deny by default.

snippets.library: an ownership check scoped to the current user
snippets.secure:
invoice = get_object_or_404(Invoice, id=request.GET['id'], owner=request.user)
return render(invoice)
snippets.note: Fetching by id alone is IDOR — any user reads any invoice. Scope the query to the authenticated owner (or check a permission), and 404 when it doesn't match.
Bind the lookup to req.user (from the verified session/token), not to any id or role the client sends. Do this on writes too, not just reads.

snippets.vulnerable: @GetMapping("/orders/{id}") Order get(@PathVariable Long id){ return repo.findById(id).get(); }
snippets.note: Enforce ownership with @PreAuthorize/@PostAuthorize or an explicit check against the authenticated principal. Never trust a role or id from the request body.

references.title: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key
